concerns.text: FR-004’s inconsistency‑detection rule references a '[deferred]' confidence level when checking whether a reported effect size falls outside the derived confidence interval. Without a concrete confidence level (e.g., 95 %), the rule cannot be operationalised nor automatically verified.

FR-005 requires computing a Wilson '[deferred]' confidence interval for the overall inconsistency proportion, but the confidence level is omitted. The missing level makes the CI width undefined, preventing deterministic verification that the correct interval is produced.
